name: Haverhill Fire Department
description_short: Honolulu Fire Department Federal Credit Union (HFDFCU) is a member-owned credit union exclusively serving firefighters and their families in Hawaii, offering savings accounts, loans, credit cards, and digital banking services.
description_long:
Honolulu Fire Department Federal Credit Union (HFDFCU) is Hawaii's only fire department credit union, established to serve the unique financial needs of firefighters and their families. As a federally insured credit union (NCUA-insured), HFDFCU operates on a non-profit, member-owned model rather than pursuing shareholder profits, which typically results in more competitive rates and lower fees for members.

HFDFCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including savings accounts, certificate accounts, Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs), checking accounts with Debit Mastercard, personal loans, auto and motorcycle loans, bill consolidation loans, home equity lines of credit, and a Visa Platinum credit card with 1% unlimited cash back and no annual fee. Members also have access to RescueNet Online Banking, mobile deposit, Zelle money transfers, bill pay services, shared branch access across credit union networks, and free ATM access. The credit union provides specialized loan products for dealerships and offers member protections like free ID theft coverage, loan protection insurance, auto repair coverage, and GAP Plus vehicle coverage.

HFDFCU distinguishes itself through its firefighter-focused membership requirement and specialization in understanding the financial circumstances of emergency responders. The organization emphasizes member education through quick start guides, loan FAQs, and rate calculators. Their bill consolidation loans start at 5.99% APR with flexible terms, and their Visa card stands out in the credit union space by offering no foreign transaction fees and no balance transfer fees. The credit union maintains physical branch locations while also providing comprehensive digital banking options through their RescueNet platform.

However, membership eligibility is restricted to firefighters and their families, making this credit union inaccessible to the general public. As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, some consumers may have fewer physical branch locations compared to larger national banks, though shared branch access partially mitigates this. The website does not prominently display current interest rates for savings products or loan APRs beyond promotional offerings, requiring members to contact the institution directly for specific rate information.
services: Visa Platinum credit card with cash back rewards
Personal loans and bill consolidation loans
Auto and motorcycle loans with dealership financing
RescueNet Online Banking with bill pay and loan management
Mobile deposit and Zelle money transfers
Shared branch access and free ATM network access
Free ID theft protection, loan protection insurance, and auto repair coverage
pros: Hawaii's only fire department credit union with specialized focus on firefighters' financial needs
Visa Platinum credit card with 1% unlimited cash back, no annual fee, no foreign transaction fees, and no balance transfer fees
Bill consolidation loans available at 5.99% APR with flexible terms to consolidate higher-rate debt
Free ID theft protection included with Visa credit card or Debit Mastercard
Comprehensive digital banking through RescueNet Online, including mobile deposit, Zelle transfers, and EZPayment loan payments
Shared branch access and free ATM access across credit union networks for nationwide convenience
Member-owned, federally insured (NCUA) non-profit structure typically offers more competitive rates than for-profit banks
cons: Membership restricted to firefighters and their families, making it inaccessible to the general public
Current interest rates for savings accounts and standard loan products not displayed on homepage; requires contacting credit union directly
Limited information about membership eligibility requirements and application process on website
Physical branch locations may be limited compared to national banks, though shared branches provide some mitigation
No mention of investment products, wealth management, or business banking services on the website
best_for: Active firefighters and their immediate family members seeking specialized financial products and member-focused service
Emergency responders looking to consolidate high-interest debt with fixed-rate consolidation loans
Firefighters needing auto and motorcycle financing with flexibility for irregular income schedules
Hawaii-based fire department personnel seeking a locally-focused credit union with identity theft protection and comprehensive digital banking
diagnosis: HFDFCU is best for active firefighters and their families in Hawaii seeking competitive rates, specialized products, and member-focused service from a non-profit credit union. The main caveat is that membership is restricted to fire department personnel and their families, making it inaccessible to the general public, and prospective members must verify eligibility before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Haverhill Fire Department — NCUA-insured credit union in Haverhill, MA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
